# feat(ml): resolve_torch_wheel() helper + collapse [dl-gpu] alias

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `3f0abbb1a796` — feat(ml): resolve_torch_wheel() helper + collapse [dl-gpu] alias

**Body**:

Phase 4 — "torch wheel resolution" and "extras collapse" items from
workspaces/kailash-ml-audit/analysis/gap-analysis.md:235-236.

resolve_torch_wheel(accelerator, *, cuda_version=None, rocm_version=None)
returns the recommended pip install recipe for any of the 6 supported
backends (cuda, mps, rocm, xpu, tpu, cpu). It is plain-data — does NOT
execute pip — so callers can print the `command` field or parse
`package` + `extra_index_url` independently (requirements.txt generation,
uv install invocations, docs examples).

Output schema:
  {
      "accelerator": "cuda",
      "package": "kailash-ml[dl-gpu]",
      "extra_index_url": "https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu124",
      "command": "<python> -m pip install 'kailash-ml[dl-gpu]' --extra-index-url ...",
      "notes": "CUDA 12.4 — cu124 wheel"
  }

CUDA auto-detects via detect_cuda_version() when not explicit; ROCm
requires explicit version (no reliable auto-detect on AMD hosts); MPS
/ CPU / XPU use the default torch wheel; TPU adds torch_xla.

Extras collapse: `[dl-gpu]` was a verbatim copy of `[dl]` + onnxruntime-gpu,
which guaranteed the two would drift. It is now a back-compat alias:
  dl-gpu = ["kailash-ml[dl]", "onnxruntime-gpu>=1.17"]

Existing `kailash-ml[dl-gpu]` invocations resolve identically. The GPU torch
wheel is selected via `pip --extra-index-url` (see `kailash-ml-gpu-setup` or
the new programmatic helper).

10 new unit tests in test_gpu_setup.py::TestResolveTorchWheel covering:
- unknown accelerator rejection
- cpu / mps / xpu / tpu default-wheel paths
- cuda with explicit, detected, and fallback versions
- rocm default + explicit version paths

Verified: 910 passed, 8 skipped on packages/kailash-ml/tests (+10 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
